scope_note: QA Fibonacci Matrix Orbit Periods cert [291]. Primary sources: Wall, D.D. (1960) Fibonacci primitive roots and the period of the Fibonacci sequence modulo a prime, American Mathematical Monthly 67(6):525-532 DOI 10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Pisano period pi(m) = order of Fibonacci matrix in GL(2,Z/mZ)); Wildberger, N.J. (2005) Divine Proportions Wild Egg Books ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8 (QA T-operator, BEDA tuples, orbit classification). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): For QA mod m=9, the Fibonacci matrix M=[[0,1],[1,1]] acting on (Z/9Z)^2 by (b,e)->M*(b,e) has order exactly 24 in GL(2,Z/9Z) (= Pisano period pi(9)); no proper divisor of 24 is the order. The three orbit types partition {1,...,9}^2 exactly: (1) Singularity: {(9,9)}, period 1, M*(0,0)=(0,0) mod 9; (2) Satellite: {(b,e): 3|b AND 3|e} \ {(9,9)}, 8 states, period 8 each; (3) Cosmos: remaining 72 states, period 24 each. Five-families alignment: Fibonacci/Lucas/Phibonacci sequence pairs have period 24 (Cosmos); Tribonacci period 8 (Satellite); Ninbonacci period 1 (Singularity). Exhaustive: all 81 states checked. GCD-3 STRUCTURAL REQUIREMENT (extension, verified not proved-in-general): the Satellite characterization {3|b AND 3|e} is a genuine subgroup of (Z/mZ)^2 only when 3|m (proved in general); for gcd(m,3)=1 the literal predicate is not closed under mod-m addition, and the Satellite (period-8) class is empty for the 8 tested moduli m in {7,10,11,13,14,16,17,20}, with the orbit-period spectrum instead following a richer m-specific divisor lattice of pi(m). The period8_fixed system reduces to a determinant-(-5) system with only the trivial solution when gcd(m,5)=1 (6 of the 8 moduli); for m=10,20 (5|m), emptiness was confirmed only by exhaustive enumeration -- the 5|m closed-form case was an open gap, now CLOSED by cert [533] (QA Orbit Satellite Ramification Cert): x^2-x-1's discriminant 5 vanishes mod 5, giving M mod 5 a repeated eigenvalue (Jordan block) whose only possible periods are {1,4,20}; by CRT with the 2-part of m (periods {1,3} for m=10, {1,3,6} for m=20), no lcm combination ever equals 8, so the Satellite class is empty for m=10,20 by a closed-form argument, not just enumeration. [533] separately proves the same mod-5 ramification is why qa_orbit_rules.py's divisor-shortcut Satellite predicate under-counts by exactly 32 whenever 3|m AND 5|m -- a related but distinct predicate from this cert's {3|b,3|e} characterization, sharing the same discriminant-5 root cause. This finding is suggestive of, not proved identical to, the mechanism cert [515] (QA Orbit-Lattice Mod-3 Collapse) proves for its own qa_step NTRU-coefficient recursion: both share the same mod-3-invertibility root cause, but [515] does not use this cert's Fibonacci matrix M, so the two Satellite/weakness mechanisms are not shown to be the same object. Theorem NT: matrix entries and orbit computations are integer arithmetic; no float state. Mechanism chain: cert [289] (Koenig Pell Ford); cert [290] (Classical Subfamily Ford Cusps); cert [281] (QA Pisano-Orbit Correspondence); cert [515] (QA Orbit-Lattice Mod-3 Collapse, shared mod-3-invertibility root cause). Lineage: derived 2026-06-01; GCD-3 extension added 2026-07-07.
